PLAIN_ENGLISH_EXPLANATION
Date of Exit — pulled from Attendance sheet column K (DOE). If no resignation, cell is manually set to 'No'.
Pulled from Attendance sheet column BB (Total Payable Days). Formula there: Total_Days − (UL + Leave_Balance_LOP). The constant 28 is the divisor (stored in cell O1).
Fixed slab salary for the employee category. Anchor = ₹12,360; AMIA / Asset = ₹17,000.
Prorated salary. Divisor is always 28 (Feb month days, stored in cell O1). Rounded to whole rupee by downstream formulas.
Basic component of salary. For standard Anchors = 100% of current salary. For AMIA/Asset = 90% (HRA is the remaining 10%).
House Rent Allowance. Zero for standard Anchors. 10% of current month salary for AMIA/Asset employees.
Leave Travel Allowance — currently zero for all employees.
Special Allowances — currently zero for all employees.
Gross Salary = sum of all salary components, rounded to nearest rupee. For standard Anchors this equals CurrentMonthSalary. For AMIA/Asset: Basic (90%) + HRA (10%) = CurrentMonthSalary.

column2:
= 12360 − GrossSalary          [if resigned & no bonus exclusion]
= 0                            [active employees]
= 0                            [resigned with exclusion flag]
column3: Top-up payment for resigned employees who worked most of the month (typically DOE ≥ 15th). Makes their total gross equal to the full standard month salary (₹12,360 for Anchors). Manually overridden to 0 where HR decides not to pay.
Statutory gratuity for employees with ≥ 5 completed years of service. Formula: ROUND(LastDrawnBasic × 15 / 26 × CompletedYears, 0). Zero for active employees or resigned with < 5 years.
Total gross payment: salary + bonus (if any) + gratuity (if any).

column2:
= ROUND(Basic + LTA + SpecialAllowances − HRA, 0)
= ROUND(GrossSalary − HRA, 0)
column3: PF-eligible gross: Gross minus HRA (HRA is excluded from PF basis). For standard Anchors = GrossSalary (since HRA = 0).
EPF contribution ceiling: capped at ₹15,000. If PF-eligible gross exceeds ₹15,000, EPF is calculated on ₹15,000 only.
Employee EPF contribution at 12%. Capped at ₹1,800 (12% of ₹15,000) when gross exceeds the ceiling.
Voluntary Provident Fund — zero for all employees.
Employee ESI contribution at 0.75%. Applied on Gross (excl. gratuity). Zero if standard salary > ₹21,000 (ESIC exemption threshold). Uses ROUNDUP (rounds fractions up, not to nearest).
Employer Pension contribution (EPS) at 8.33% of EPF wages. Derived from employee PF amount: employee_PF ÷ 12% × 8.33%. Capped because PF is already capped at ₹15,000 ceiling.
Employer PF contribution at 3.67% (= 12% employee − 8.33% pension). The remaining employer share after pension allocation.
Employer ESI contribution at 3.25% on Gross (excl. gratuity). Zero if standard salary exceeds ₹21,000 ESIC exemption limit. Note: NOT rounded (unlike employee ESI which uses ROUNDUP).
Tax Deducted at Source — entered manually for employees with TDS liability.
Any other deductions or advances — entered manually.
Insurance advance recovery — entered manually.
State Profession Tax (Karnataka/Telangana slab): ₹200 if gross ≥ ₹20,001; ₹150 if gross ≥ ₹15,001; ₹0 otherwise. Applied on Gross excluding gratuity.
Labour Welfare Fund deduction — zero for all employees currently.
Total of statutory employee deductions: PF + ESI + LWF.
Net take-home salary: Final Gross minus all deductions. Rounded to nearest rupee.
Completed years of service, rounded to nearest year. ROUND(..., 0) means ≥ 6 months of a year counts as a full year (Indian Gratuity Act). Used only for resigned employees.
Last drawn basic salary — taken as the standard monthly salary (₹12,360 for Anchor; ₹15,300 for AMIA/Asset).
Statutory gratuity: (Basic ÷ 26) × 15 × Years. Only for resigned employees with ≥ 5 completed years. ₹0 for active employees or those with < 5 years.
